actions.text: ### Key Decisions and Agreements
actions.scheduling_reasoning: Normal priority tasks scheduled in 13-16:00 window

actions.text: **Abandon product diversification strategy** - Vrijen came in proposing to build side revenue streams (VC productivity suite, Logan's content kits) while maintaining Careerspan in "stasis mode." Laura firmly redirected: "We go furthest fastest when we niche down" and challenged the creative diversification as risky. Vrijen acknowledged this and pivoted to focusing purely on Careerspan's core value proposition. **Why it matters:** This recenters the company on its fundamental strength (career repository + AI structuring) rather than fragmenting limited resources across multiple products.
actions.scheduling_reasoning: Normal priority tasks scheduled in 13-16:00 window

actions.text: **Repository-as-core-value clarity** - Laura articulated what makes Careerspan compelling: "It's my repository of my total skill set that my frail human brain can't remember. I'm investing, I'm creating all this content." This framing shifts the positioning from "job matching platform" (which nobody believes works) to "personal career intelligence system." **Why it matters:** This is the defensible moat and the reason users return - they've invested cognitive work that becomes more valuable over time.
actions.scheduling_reasoning: Normal priority tasks scheduled in 13-16:00 window

actions.text: **Partnership ecosystem as primary growth driver** - Laura strongly advocated for "going shopping for a real partner" rather than DIY distribution. She cited a Seattle competitor who "got married" to a compatible partner during tech winter and survived. Specifically mentioned Yoodly (interview prep platform) as potential integration partner. **Why it matters:** Accelerates distribution without burning runway on sales infrastructure; leverages existing user bases.
actions.scheduling_reasoning: Normal priority tasks scheduled in 13-16:00 window

actions.text: **Three-way conversation scheduled** - Laura offered to have a strategic session with both Vrijen and Logan to discuss "short and medium strategy" for solving runway challenges. Vrijen accepted enthusiastically. **Why it matters:** Brings Logan into the strategic realignment conversation; Laura is investing advisory time to help them navigate the pivot.
actions.scheduling_reasoning: Normal priority tasks scheduled in 13-16:00 window

actions.text: ### Strategic Context
actions.scheduling_reasoning: Normal priority tasks scheduled in 13-16:00 window

actions.text: Positioning Shift:** Vrijen is experiencing founder burnout after years of trying to build a VC-backable company. The original plan (in-house team, employer revenue model) has proven slow and capital-intensive. Two UK competitors have launched similar products, creating urgency around market capture. The company is out of runway and needs either revenue or a sustainable path forward without dilutive fundraising.
actions.scheduling_reasoning: Normal priority tasks scheduled in 13-16:00 window

actions.text: Pain Points Surfaced:**
actions.scheduling_reasoning: Normal priority tasks scheduled in 13-16:00 window

actions.text: VC funding model misalignment with actual product-market fit (candidates love it, employers are hard to monetize)
actions.scheduling_reasoning: Normal priority tasks scheduled in 13-16:00 window

actions.text: Fatigue with "building the wrong way" - acknowledges starting "ass backwards"
actions.scheduling_reasoning: Normal priority tasks scheduled in 13-16:00 window

actions.text: Tension between populist mission (helping candidates) and B2B revenue pressure

actions.text: We're abandoning the productivity suite and content kit ideas. You were right: getting creative during runway crunch is how companies lose the plot.

actions.text: We're recentering on the core capability: unstructured input → structured career intelligence (the thing that caught your attention on day zero).
